reason: Both answers correctly lead with alert hygiene over rotation changes and give similar concrete audit steps (review alerts, delete/downgrade noise, route non-urgent to business hours, make paging rare). Both miss the point about reducing sprint commitment during on-call week. B is slightly stronger because it explicitly calls for weekly review of new alerts to prevent noise creep (a named key point) and explicitly addresses why follow-the-sun isn't the fix at 6 people, whereas A omits the follow-the-sun discussion entirely and only implies periodic review via a noise-triggered budget. The difference is modest but B covers more of the intended key points without adding unnecessary length.
